your_book:
Tuesday was a rough session for Jaleel's book, which sits at $21,156 with an unrealized loss of $602 (‑2.77% overall). The damage was broad but concentrated in the larger positions. Adobe (ADBE, 16.8% weight) shed another 3.23% today, deepening its position loss to ‑7.4% — the narrative headwind around AI disruption (Claude Design, CFO exit) continues to overwhelm fundamentals that, by the data, still look solid. Danaher (DHR) was the session's real shock, cratering 10.99% on what appears to be an earnings-related catalyst (the headline links DHR to a 3M/crude oil day, suggesting sector-specific news drove the move); the position is now down 0.3% from cost on a name carried as Strong Buy with a QARP of 72.9 — that gap between price action and conviction deserves scrutiny. ServiceNow (NOW, 11.1%) dropped 2.52% ahead of its earnings tomorrow (July 22), which is the real event to watch.

On the positive side, Rocket Lab (RKLB) bounced 5.14% but remains deeply underwater at ‑24.4% — and critically, it carries an AVOID verdict and the book's lowest QARP score (43.9). A one-day pop in a structurally misaligned position isn't a thesis repair. NVDA (+1.97%) and AVGO (+2.21%) provided modest offsets, and Neurocrine (NBIX, +3.5%) was the quiet outperformer.

The drift story here is threefold: ADBE is the largest weight and the thesis (quality compounder) is being actively contested by the market; RKLB sits at nearly 10% of the book with an AVOID flag; and BSX reports July 29 carrying both a guidance-slash flag and a ‑9.8% open loss. Jaleel's book is capital-preservation oriented — those three deserve decisions, not patience by default.

attention.headline: Danaher down ~11% in a single session — what just broke?
attention.note: A 10.99% single-day drop on a Strong Buy/QARP 72.9 name demands an immediate read of the catalyst: if it's an earnings miss or guidance cut, the thesis needs re-evaluation before assuming mean reversion.

attention.headline: Earnings tomorrow (Jul 22) — largest single weight after ADBE
attention.note: NOW reports July 22 and already dropped 2.52% today; at 11.1% of the book with a high-multiple, macro-sensitive profile, a miss or cautious guide could inflict meaningful portfolio-level damage overnight.

attention.headline: AVOID-rated name at 9.1% weight, ‑24.4% in the hole, bouncing on no thesis repair
attention.note: Today's 5.14% pop doesn't change the AVOID verdict or the QARP of 43.9 — at nearly a tenth of a capital-preservation book, the position size and the rating are in direct conflict.

attention.headline: Largest holding down 3.23% today, ‑7.4% open loss, narrative still deteriorating
attention.note: With 16.8% of the book in ADBE and price continuing to diverge from the Strong Buy thesis, the AI-disruption narrative risk is compounding — next catalyst isn't until September 10, leaving a long window of exposure.
